name: Buf (Modern Protobuf Toolchain)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Buf Technologies; BSR (Buf Schema Registry) is commercial

auth.notes: BSR (schema registry) requires API key; local buf lint/generate is free

performance.notes: Protobuf compilation; significantly faster than raw protoc for large schemas

agent_readiness.notes: Modern Protobuf toolchain replacing protoc. buf lint (style checking), buf format, buf generate (code gen with buf.gen.yaml), buf breaking (backwards compatibility). buf.yaml module configuration. BSR (Buf Schema Registry) for proto dependency management. Connect-RPC code generation. Works with protoc plugins (go, java, typescript, etc.). Eliminates protoc binary management complexity. Linting enforces Google/Uber style guides. The standard for serious Protobuf projects.

gotchas: Learning curve if migrating from protoc
BSR is commercial for private repos
buf.gen.yaml plugin configuration can be complex
Breaking change detection may flag intentional changes
best_when: Teams wanting lint/format/breaking-change detection for protos
Replacing raw protoc with managed dependency and plugin execution
avoid_when: Simple one-time protoc usage (buf adds configuration overhead)
Projects where BSR private repos cost is a concern
summary: Buf is the modern Protobuf toolchain replacing raw protoc. buf lint, format, generate, breaking (backwards-compat check). buf.gen.yaml for codegen plugins across Go/Java/TypeScript etc. BSR for schema registry and dependency management. Eliminates protoc binary management. Standard for serious gRPC/Protobuf projects. BSR private repos are commercial.
